title: Innodata (INOD) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
text: Innodata delivered exceptional Q1 2026 results, with revenue reaching $90.1 million, representing 54% year-over-year growth and exceeding analyst consensus by $13.6 million. The company achieved an adjusted gross margin of 47%, surpassing its 40% target by 7 percentage points, while adjusted EBITDA hit $25 million (28% of revenue)—139% above consensus expectations. The company ended the quarter with $117.4 million in cash, up $35.1 million sequentially, maintaining a fully undrawn $50 million credit facility. Net income reached $14.9 million with diluted EPS of $0.42, far exceeding the $0.08 consensus estimate. Management announced a significant engagement with a major technology company potentially generating $51 million in revenue annually, positioning this customer as the company's expected second-largest by year-end. Notably, this customer contributed zero revenue just twelve months prior. The company raised full-year 2026 revenue guidance to approximately 40% or more, up from the previous 35% guidance. Revenue diversification is accelerating, with aggregate revenue from non-primary tech customers growing 453% year-over-year. Key initiatives include trust and safety services selection as a global partner, beta release of an agent evaluation and observability platform resulting in a $1 million engagement, and a prime contract award under the Missile Defense Agency's SHIELD program. Two papers were accepted at ICML 2026, with one receiving prestigious Spotlight designation. The company transitioned to single-segment reporting, reflecting its integrated focus on agentic AI technologies across all business units.
